source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Hand Crush Injury During Drill Pipe Transfer
actions_taken: Injured person evacuated via helicopter at 08:05 hours
Injured person returned to rig on restricted work duty
Maintenance supervisor inspected throttle pedal following incident
event.summary: On November 26, 2015 at 01:31 hours, a Floor Hand on the Rowan Gorilla IV suffered a laceration to his left hand and wrist when a stand of 4" drill pipe swung uncontrolled and crushed his hand between the pipe and an iron roughneck. The Assistant Driller (AD) was raising the top drive to pick up drill pipe when he noticed the throttle pedal felt different, looked down briefly, and failed to stop the operation. During this lapse in attention, the drill pipe swung freely and struck the Floor Hand. The incident was caused by lack of focus by the AD and failure of the crew to follow the Red Zone Policy.

hazards.name: Uncontrolled movement of suspended drill pipe
Personnel in hazardous zone during equipment operation

threats.name: Loss of operator attention during hoisting operation
Throttle pedal malfunction causing unintended acceleration
Failure to implement Red Zone exclusion policy

consequences.name: Lost work time and restricted duty assignment
Potential for more severe injury or fatality

controls.name: Standard procedure requiring top drive to stop and wait for floor hands to tail-in drill pipe
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to prevent uncontrolled pipe movement

supporting_text: The normal procedure for running drill pipe called for the top drive to be raised and to stop and wait for the floor hands to be ready to 'tail-in' the drill pipe into place.

The AD took his eyes and attention off of the operation, and during this lapse in attention to the job, he broke from normal procedure by continuing to raise the top drive when he should have stopped and waited for the Floor Hands to move the drill pipe.

controls.name: Red Zone Policy - exclusion of personnel from hazardous area during equipment operation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to keep personnel out of harm's way during equipment movement

supporting_text: the crew on the drill floor failed to follow Rowan's 'Red Zone Policy'. This policy states that no personnel should be in the 'Red Zone' while the top drive is being operated.
Had the drill crew followed these policies, the incident would likely not have occurred.

controls.name: Throttle pedal maintenance and inspection
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure proper functioning of throttle pedal

supporting_text: on the tour previous to the incident, the Driller and AD had identified a missing pin in the top drive throttle pedal. They replaced the pin with a nut and bolt, and initially the pedal did stick but after adjustment the pedal operated smoothly.
The Maintenance Supervisor inspected the pedal following the incident and determined the pedal to be working correctly.

controls.name: Operator situational awareness and attention to hoisting operation
controls.barrier_role: Human behavioral control requiring continuous focus during equipment operation

supporting_text: The primary cause of the incident was lack of attention to the job. The AD took his attention off the job while work was still on-going and this lapse in attention caused him to break from normal procedure.
He briefly looked down at the pedal, and when he looked up, the drill pipe swung uncontrolled against one of the Floor Hand's wrist.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
